name: Bundles/edge-cases
description: Bundle edge-case conformance tests, ported from the Microsoft FHIR Server E2E suite (BundleEdgeCaseTests.cs): an invalid Bundle.type value, a resource id shared between two different resource types within the same Bundle, and _history/_history-by-version GET requests nested inside a batch Bundle. Two tests from the source file are deliberately NOT ported: GivenABundleWithConditionalUpdateByReference_WhenExecutedWithMaximizedConditionalQueryParallelism_RunsTheQueryInParallel requires a server-specific 'MaximizeConditionalQueryParallelism' bundle-processing option with no TestScript equivalent, and WhenProcessingMultipleBundlesWithTheSameResource_AndIncreasingTheExpectedVersionInParallel_ThenUpdateTheResourcesAsExpected requires firing multiple concurrent HTTP requests from a single test flow, which this sequential TestScript engine has no primitive for. Uses a client-supplied id (PUT) for the _history fixture so the version-1 URL can be a static literal.

operation.description: PUT Patient/fhir262-edge-hist-p1 (client id) to deterministically create version 1 before the _history bundle test runs

test.name: Invalid Bundle.type value is rejected with 400 Bad Request
test.description: POST a Bundle whose type is the literal string 'invalid-type' (not a valid FHIR Bundle.type code) to the base URL. The server must reject it with 400 Bad Request rather than attempting to process the entries.

operation.description: POST Bundle with type='invalid-type' to the base URL

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request for an unsupported Bundle.type value
Response body should be an OperationOutcome (warningOnly: some servers return a plain error body instead)

test.name: Same literal id shared between different resource types is processed as two independent resources
test.description: POST a batch Bundle containing PUT Patient/fhir262-edge-shared-id and PUT Observation/fhir262-edge-shared-id — the same id string used for two different resource types. Because logical ids are scoped per resource type, both operations must succeed independently; the id collision must not cause either entry to fail.

operation.description: POST batch Bundle with same-id Patient and Observation PUTs

assert.description: Batch submission must return HTTP 200
Response must be a Bundle
Response Bundle must contain 2 entries
Patient/fhir262-edge-shared-id entry must succeed with a 2xx status
Observation/fhir262-edge-shared-id entry must also succeed with a 2xx status
First entry's resource id must be the shared id
Second entry's resource id must also be the shared id

test.name: Both same-id resources are independently retrievable by resource type
test.description: After the shared-id batch commits, GET Patient/fhir262-edge-shared-id and GET Observation/fhir262-edge-shared-id must each return the correct resource type — proving the id namespace is scoped per resource type, not global.

assert.description: Read must return HTTP 200
Response must be a Patient
Returned Patient id must be the shared id
Read must return HTTP 200
Response must be an Observation
Returned Observation id must be the shared id

test.name: A batch Bundle containing _history and _history/{version} GET requests returns the expected resources
test.description: POST a batch Bundle with two GET entries: Patient/{id}/_history and Patient/{id}/_history/1. Both must succeed inside the Bundle just as they would as standalone requests: the first returns a history Bundle whose newest entry is the Patient, and the second returns the version-1 Patient directly.

operation.description: POST batch Bundle with _history and _history/1 GET entries

assert.description: Batch submission must return HTTP 200
Response must be a Bundle
Response Bundle must contain 2 entries
The _history entry must succeed with a 2xx status
The _history/1 entry must succeed with a 2xx status
The _history entry's embedded resource should itself be a history-type Bundle (warningOnly: depth of nested resource-type resolution may vary by server serialization)
The _history/1 entry's embedded resource must be the version-1 Patient

operation.description: Teardown: defensive conditional delete in case a lenient server processed the invalid-type Bundle
